exemplar.title: The Mathematics of Running: Can humans break the two-hour marathon barrier?

detailedExemplarData.title: The Mathematics of Running: Can humans break the two-hour marathon barrier?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Logical overall narrative with clear introduction, body and conclusion
Effective use of headings, numbered tables and graphs to guide the reader
Concise presentation of key mathematical models and their evaluation
weaknesses: Occasional redundancy in background sections (e.g. repeated references to data sourcing)
A few figure captions and tables could be more sharply labelled or summarised
Transitions around the data-exclusion decision could be more tightly justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the strand assessments placed the work at band 3, the explorationâs structure is highly coherent, with an introduction that sets clear aims, a well-sequenced development of Models IâIII, and a concise conclusion/evaluation section. The use of consistent formatting, clear numbering of figures/tables and logical headings elevates the organization to the top of the rubric, meriting a level 4.

strengths: Consistent and accurate notation for exponential and logarithmic models throughout
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, scatter plots, fitted curves, algebraic derivations)
Clear step-by-step explanations of conversions, derivatives and prediction calculations
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in axis-labelling across different graphs
Occasional duplicate expressions (e.g. repeated derivative formula) that could be streamlined
A couple of symbol definitions (e.g. Î¸ in Graph 2) were not fully explicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a very clear logical structure in communicating mathematics: notation is almost entirely consistent, definitions are given, and multiple complementary representations reinforce each other. Although minor lapses occur, the overall mathematical communication is of excellent clarity and coherence, justifying a band 4.

strengths: Integration of personal running background and âRunnemathicsâ framing shows genuine engagement
Choice of real historical data and cross-disciplinary physiological research (C =118) evidences independent initiative
Extension proposals (female data, different models, other sports) reflect proactive thinking
weaknesses: Modelling strategy (exponential regression and differentiation) follows common IA conventions
Personal narrative could be deepened with more on why specific parameter choices reflect the authorâs own perspective
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation clearly bears the studentâs personal stamp through contextual framing and model choices. However, the approach remains largely within typical IA conventions, and creative aspects, while clearly present, do not reach the outstanding level required for band 3, so band 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflections on data exclusion, model limitations and derivative interpretations
Thoughtful critique of asymptote realism and suggestions for piecewise or error-metric analysis
Clear evaluation of outcomes with forward-looking extension ideas
weaknesses: Reflections are intermittent rather than woven continuously through every section
Quantitative error analysis (e.g. residual comparison) is suggested but not carried out
Deeper statistical diagnostics (e.g. heteroscedasticity, confidence intervals) are not explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although some reflections occur only at key junctures, they are substantial and critically analyze model suitability, limitations and implications. This level of insight and evaluation exceeds basic reflection, aligning with band 3.

strengths: Appropriate and correctly applied exponential regression, logarithmic manipulation and first-order calculus
Manual derivation of derivatives and algebraic solutions for Model III are logically sequenced and error-free
Use of Pearsonâs r and curve-fit diagnostics to assess model quality
weaknesses: Minor rounding inconsistencies in reported correlation coefficients
Some graph axis-labelling inconsistencies do not affect mathematical validity but interrupt precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematical techniques are fully commensurate with SL level and are applied correctly and thoroughly. The student shows a solid understanding of regression, logarithmic solving and differentiation, with clear workings. Minor notational slips do not detract from the overall robust and accurate use of mathematics, meriting band 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations generally follow a logical sequence and are clear to a mathematically-literate reader.  Occasional digressions (e.g. lengthy historical commentary mid-analysis) slightly cloud clarity, hence âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ logical communication.

Workings are shown, algebraic manipulation is correct, and derivative reasoning is accurate.  Minor inconsistencies (axis labels, rounding) exist but do not materially affect validity, indicating good but not perfect understanding â hence band 4.

The student employs tables, scatter plots with curve fits, algebraic manipulation and derivatives.  These multiple representations meaningfully reinforce each other, though some graph axes are mis-labelled.  Effectiveness is good but not flawless, matching band 3.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: rationale, data collection, three successive models, analysis, and evaluation. Each section logically builds on the previous one, so the reader can trace how conclusions arise from evidence.  Minor logical breaks remain â e.g. the sudden exclusion of pre-1944 data is stated but not fully justified mathematically â yet overall flow is good rather than exemplary, matching band 3.

The student chooses to compare several models, differentiate the best-fit function, and set their own asymptoteâall showing creativity and initiative above minimal expectations, but not reaching the exceptional breadth required for band 3.

The student provides thoughtful analysis of model suitability, correlation coefficients, and derivative behaviour.  The critique identifies weaknesses (asymptote realism, outliers, piecewise possibility) but stops short of deep statistical diagnostics, so band 2 is appropriate.

Outcomes are evaluated by comparing predicted years and asymptotes, and by suggesting extensions.  Evaluation is clear but not comprehensive (e.g. no quantitative error measures), aligning with band 2.

Reflection is integrated at key stages (why data pre-1944 was excluded, how derivative informs plateau, discussion of limitations).  These reflections are meaningful but intermittent rather than âsubstantial throughoutâ, fitting band 2.

Standard notation (e.g. t= Ae^{Bx}+C, ln, derivative prime) is used correctly and symbols are defined.  Minor slips such as inconsistent variable naming (year sometimes x, sometimes labelled on y-axis) prevent full consistency, so âmostly appropriateâ (band 3) is justified.

The narrative links the investigation to the studentâs personal running background and coins âRunnemathicsâ, demonstrating a clear personal approach.  Still, much of the write-up follows standard IA format without innovative features, suiting band 2.

Most material directly supports the modelling task; however, extended narrative about Kipchoge and several repeated explanations of exponential behaviour add length without mathematical value.  Content is therefore âmostly relevant and concise,â consistent with band 3 rather than the very highest level.

Exponential regression, logarithmic manipulation and first-order calculus are all directly relevant to modelling marathon performance and are applied appropriately, though the mathematics remains at a moderate SL level.  This matches ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ (band 3).

Selecting, cleaning and modelling real performance data and manually deriving Model III show significant personal initiative beyond textbook examples.  Nonetheless, the strategy (basic exponential regression) is common and not deeply original, so evidence is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ (band 2).

Headings, numbered tables and graphs, and a standard introduction-body-conclusion structure evidence solid organisation.  Transitions are generally smooth, but some tables/figures (e.g. Graph 1 caption) are cropped and a few pages repeat similar commentary, so the organisation is clear but not âexceptional,â warranting band 3.

All techniques (curve fitting, solving ln-equations, derivatives) lie comfortably within the AA SL syllabus and are executed without unnecessary simplification, satisfying âfully commensurate with course levelâ (band 4).

annotations.comment: Identification of the negative exponent in Model Iâs function is clear and shows accurate understanding of exponential decay.
Reporting Pearsonâs r = â0.9514 for Model II demonstrates effective use of statistical measures to evaluate fit.
The introduction effectively humanizes the investigation through âRunnemathicsâ, showing genuine personal connection and creative framing of the research.
The aim statement repeats earlier phrasing and could be sharpened to explicitly state the mathematical objectives and criteria for success.
The introduction contains lengthy background on Kipchoge; consider condensing to focus on how these details inform your modelling assumptions.
The rationale describes data sourcing twice; streamline to avoid redundancy and improve flow.
Notation in the general model $$t = Ae^{-Bx} + C$$ is clear and consistent, aiding reader comprehension.
Axis labelling is reversed here: you state x-axis is time and y-axis is year, but earlier graphs use the opposite. Ensure consistent axis definitions.
Table 1 displays a century of data in four columns, which overwhelms the reader. Consider splitting or summarising key points graphically.
The step-by-step conversion from hh:mm:ss to decimal minutes is clearly shown, demonstrating strong attention to mathematical detail.
The choice to exclude pre-1944 data is asserted but not backed by a quantitative analysis of residuals; include a comparison to justify this split.
Your critique of Model Iâs asymptote (124.4 min) as unrealistic is insightful and shows critical reflection on model limitations.
Model I uses relevant calculus and regression techniques appropriately, reflecting solid application of SL-level mathematics.
Table 2 spans irregular record intervals; comment on how gaps (e.g. 1909â1913) might skew an exponential fit.
The outline of assumptions is logically structured, but transitions between points could be more concise to aid reader navigation.
Graph 2 introduces Î¸ as 1900=Î¸ without explicit definition; clarify notation for first-time symbols.
When citing the Guardian prediction, briefly outline its methodology to reinforce credibility of your assumption.
Algebraic steps solving for A and B in Model III are logically sequenced, demonstrating strong use of simultaneous equations.
Graph 3 would be clearer if the caption included a figure number and a brief description of axes.
The manual derivation of the first derivative for Model II is correct and well explained, showing thorough understanding.
The derivative expression appears twice identically; remove duplicate to streamline mathematical communication.
The integration of external physiological predictions to set C = 118 shows strong personal initiative and cross-disciplinary research.
The transformation from ln45.7âlnA to GDC inputs omits an intermediate explanation; include a note on how you handle the non-linear system.
Discussing historical events (e.g. WWII) alongside data trend adds valuable context and shows independent analytical thinking.
Minor inconsistency: earlier you reference r=â0.9514, here r=â0.9154. Ensure correlation coefficients are consistently reported.
The substitution of t=120 and t=119 into Model II demonstrates adept manipulation of logarithms to predict future years.
When presenting predicted years, restate x in calendar years explicitly to ensure reader clarity.
The evaluation merges conclusion and extension in one narrative; consider structuring separate sections for conclusion, evaluation and extensions.
Reflection on limitations is thorough, but could be strengthened with quantitative error metrics to support your critique.
The extension ideas (female data, other sports, different models) are highly relevant and show proactive thinking about future research.
